title: The Epitaph
content:
Here, five feet deep, lies on his back
    A cobbler, starmonger, and quack;
    Who to the stars, in pure good will,
    Does to his best look upward still.
    Weep, all you customers that use
    His pills, his almanacks, or shoes;
    And you that did your fortunes seek,
    Step to his grave but once a-week;
    This earth, which bears his body's print,
    You'll find has so much virtue in't,
    That I durst pawn my ears, 'twill tell
    Whate'er concerns you full as well,
    In physic, stolen goods, or love,
    As he himself could, when above.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Jonathan Swift, titled "The Epitaph", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
